name: Garonne Hillsides Loop from Tonneins
name_original: Coteaux de Garonne — boucle au départ de Tonneins
description: This difficult 26.5-mile touring cycling loop from Tonneins offers challenging climbs and scenic views of the Garonne hillsides.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tonneins are situated on the right bank of the Garonne River in the Lot-et-Garonne department of France. The region features diverse landscapes, including riverbanks, hillsides, and the serene Canal Latéral à la Garonne. Tonneins serves as a hub for several significant cycling routes, such as the Canal de Garonne greenway and the Vallée du Lot cycle route. The area's infrastructure supports various cycling experiences, from well-paved paths to some unpaved sections along the canal. Best touring cycling routes around Tonneins The most popular touring cycling route is Garonne Hillsides Loop from Tonneins, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the elevated terrain surrounding the Garonne. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Baïse Aqueduct – Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Tonneins, a difficult 74.8 miles (120.4 km) path. This route combines scenic waterways with the architectural feature of the Baïse Aqueduct. Local touring cyclists also love the Garonne Lateral Canal – Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Tonneins, a 38.0 miles (61.2 km) trail leading through canal paths and past an aqueduct, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Tonneins is defined by the Garonne River, the Canal Latéral à la Garonne, and varied rural landscapes, including hillsides. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Tonneins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tonneins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tonneins?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Tonneins, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Tonneins?
A: The terrain around Tonneins is diverse, featuring riverbanks, hillsides, and the serene Canal Latéral à la Garonne. While many routes offer well-paved surfaces, particularly along the Garonne River, some segments along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne may feature unpaved or bumpy sections due to tree roots. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287007615/garonne-hillsides-loop-from-tonneins" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Hillsides Loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt; explore more elevated terrain, while canal paths are generally flatter.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Tonneins?
A: Yes, Tonneins offers routes suitable for various ability levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region's commitment to 'environmental friendly tourism' ensures options for all. The flat, shaded paths along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne are generally more accessible and pleasant for families or those seeking an easier ride.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Tonneins?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Tonneins are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287007615/garonne-hillsides-loop-from-tonneins" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Hillsides Loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37721354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baïse Aqueduct – Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40952845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal – Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: The Garonne River is a central feature, with a picturesque 2 km promenade along its quays in Tonneins. You can also encounter the distinctive natural rocky bar of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Roches de Reculay&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt; itself is a beautiful natural and man-made feature, offering serene waterways and shaded paths. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rembrandt’s Christ on the Cross in Saint-Vincent Church, Le Mas-d’Agenais&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4944373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Clairac&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Canal Latéral à la Garonne?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt; is a major cycling artery in the region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40952845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal – Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Écluse de la Gaule – Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Tonneins&lt;/a&gt; specifically incorporate sections along this scenic waterway, offering pleasant, shaded cycling.

Q: Where can I park my car to start a touring cycling route in Tonneins?
A: Tonneins is a cyclist-friendly town, and you can typically find parking in and around the town center, especially near the Garonne quays or public spaces. Many routes, being loops, start and end conveniently in Tonneins, making it easy to access your vehicle.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tonneins?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Tonneins, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. The town even hosts an annual cycling rally in the spring, indicating favorable conditions during this season. Summers can be warm, but the shaded paths along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne provide some relief.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Tonneins?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tonneins are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the serene paths along the Garonne River and Canal Latéral à la Garonne, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there accommodations in Tonneins that cater specifically to cyclists?
A: Yes, the Tonneins area is known for its cyclist-friendly infrastructure. Many guesthouses and accommodations carry the 'accueil vélo' label, indicating they provide amenities tailored for cyclists, such as secure bike storage, charging facilities for e-bikes, and repair kits.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling routes that pass through Tonneins?
A: Tonneins is strategically positioned at the convergence of several significant long-distance cycling routes. It lies at the intersection of the Canal de Garonne greenway and the Vallée du Lot cycle route, and serves as a crossroads for major itineraries like the Canal des 2 Mers, the Scandibérique, the Vallée du Dropt, and the 'So vélo' route connecting Marmande and Casteljaloux.
tips: The Bourdeille mill, known as the Gibra mill, benefits from the prevailing winds coming from the west on its mound at the top of a hill. From Pech-de-Bère in the south – where the Lot flows into the Garonne – to the town of Marmande in the northwest, it offers you from its privileged location a profound panorama of the Garonne valley. Listed in the inventory of Historic Monuments in 1979, the Gibra windmill benefited from a restoration in the early 1980s, a perfect example of safeguarding local heritage. It has all the equipment for turning in the wind and grinding grain, and on windy days (and open to the public), you can watch milling demonstrations.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult touring bicycle ride. It requires a very good level of fitness due to its significant elevation gain of nearly 490 meters over 42.6 kilometers. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists or those seeking a strenuous workout.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Coteaux de Garonne — loop from Tonneins?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily paved, consisting largely of roads (41.9 km), with smaller sections of streets, paths, and access roads. While the surface type is generally smooth, the challenge comes from the substantial elevation changes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 42.6-kilometer loop is approximately 2 hours and 46 minutes. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque landscapes characteristic of the Lot-et-Garonne region. The route likely offers scenic views of the surrounding countryside, including vast panoramas over the Garonne and Lot valleys, the Marmandais and Pays de Serres hills, and local vineyards.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a few interesting spots. You'll encounter the Gibra Mill and the Moulin de Grateloup. Additionally, the historic ruins of Saint-Gayrand Church are also on the loop.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling loop in Tonneins?
faqs.answer: As Tonneins is the starting and ending point for this loop, you should look for public parking options within the town. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but general town parking would be your best bet to begin the ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially colder, wetter conditions of winter. This will make the significant climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is not recommended for families or beginners. Its classification as a 'difficult' ride with significant elevation gain means it requires a very good level of fitness and cycling experience. Flatter, more accessible routes like the Canal des Deux Mers greenway would be more suitable for those seeking an easier ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this public road-based cycling route, it's important to consider the safety of your pet given the route's difficulty and the presence of vehicle traffic. If you choose to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, on a leash, and capable of handling the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Coteaux de Garonne — loop from Tonneins. It utilizes public roads and paths, making it freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with a few other paths. You will find small sections that overlap with the Véloroute de Tonneins à Clairac, Chemin de la Roche, Rue de la Passerelle, and Chemin de Losse.
